description:
You are a junior backup-operator who has just received a small batch of backup-metadata files in JSON format.  
Your mission is to perform a pre-restore sanity-check on every JSON document found in  

/home/user/backup_sim/backups/  

and then produce two machine-readable artefacts that the senior operator (and our automated tests) will later inspect.

Step-by-step objectives
1. JSON-schema validation  
   • The canonical schema is stored in  
     /home/user/backup_sim/schema/backup_schema.json  
   • Check every file in /home/user/backup_sim/backups/ against that schema.  
   • The check must be carried out **one file at a time** so that an individual PASS/FAIL result can be captured for each file.  
   • Any validation method available on a typical Linux CLI is acceptable (jq, a Python snippet, ajv-cli, etc.) as long as the required report described below is produced.

2. Generate the validation report  
   • Create a new directory  
     /home/user/backup_sim/validation/  
     if it does not yet exist.  
   • Inside that directory write a plain text file named  
     validation_report.log  
   • The file must contain:  
     – One result line per JSON file, sorted alphabetically by *file name* (not by full path).  
     – The exact line format our tests will look for is:  
       <filename>: <OK|FAIL>  
       Example →  backup_2023-11-01.json: OK  
     – After all per-file lines, append the two summary lines shown below **in exactly this order**:  
       TOTAL_OK=<number_of_successes>  
       TOTAL_FAIL=<number_of_failures>  
   • A final, correct validation_report.log produced from the sample data we shipped with the exercise would therefore look exactly like this (line breaks are significant):  

     backup_2023-11-01.json: OK  
     backup_2023-12-06.json: FAIL  
     backup_2023-12-20.json: OK  
     TOTAL_OK=2  
     TOTAL_FAIL=1  

3. Produce a list of “old” backups (pure jq processing)  
   • Any backup with backupDate **earlier than 2023-12-01** counts as “old”.  
   • Use jq to extract just the file *names* (not paths) of those old backups, and write them as a compact JSON array to  
     /home/user/backup_sim/validation/older_backups.json  
   • The resulting file must contain JSON only, no extra new-lines or comments.  
     For the data we provide the correct content is:  
     [
       "backup_2023-11-01.json"
     ]

4. Double-check permissions  
   • All files you create under /home/user/backup_sim/validation/ must be writable by the regular user (no root needed).

Important:  
• Do not remove, rename, or modify any files that already exist under /home/user/backup_sim/.  
• The automated test harness will look exclusively at the two files you have been asked to create and nothing else, so make sure their names, casing and internal formats are **exactly** as specified above.  
• Feel free to display any console output you find useful while working—only the content of validation_report.log and older_backups.json is graded.
